title: Your $80 Pill Is Identical to the $4 Generic
hook: The FDA requires generics to contain the exact same active ingredient as the brand name — but Big Pharma spends $6 billion a year making sure you don't believe it.
angle: Brand-name drug loyalty is pure marketing psychology — FDA bioequivalence law proves generics are legally the same drug, making the price gap entirely manufactured.
audience: Anyone who takes regular prescription medications and pays full retail price without asking for a generic
key_numbers: $6 billion in pharmaceutical DTC advertising per year
$600/year average American overpays on brand-name drugs
$50 billion in unnecessary brand-name spending nationally each year
